SKL_MEM_I Class Reference

#include <skl.h>

List of all members.

Public Member Functions

virtual SKL_ANY New (const size_t Size)
virtual void Delete (const SKL_ANY p, size_t Size)
void Realloc (SKL_ANY &Ptr, const size_t Size, const size_t Old_Size=0)

Detailed Description

Instance of this class should provide interface for memory allocation and disallocation. The default implementation uses malloc() / free() low-level calls.
A new-with-placement operator is defined, taking the memory pool as placement data. You can then use: new (Mem) MY_CLASS to allocate MY_CLASS on the SKL_MEM_I *Mem memory pool.
For convenience, a default global static instance of SKL_MEM_I is available as: SKL_MEM_I *SKL_MEM.

Member Function Documentation

virtual void SKL_MEM_I::Delete (  const SKL_ANY  p,
size_t  Size
)  [virtual]
 

This function should disallocate the memory segment pointed to by the 'p' parameter, and of size 'Size'.

Parameters:
p Pointer to the memory segment. This pointer should have been obtained by a call to New() member function.
Size size of the memory to be freed. This size *must* match the size that was passed to the corresponding New() call as argument.

virtual SKL_ANY SKL_MEM_I::New (  const size_t  Size  )  [virtual]
 

This function should return a pointer to a freshly allocation memory segment of at least 'Size' size. It returns 0 if allocation was not possible.

Parameters:
Size the desired size to be allocated.

void SKL_MEM_I::Realloc (  SKL_ANY &  Ptr,
const size_t  Size,
const size_t  Old_Size = 0
) 
 

This function re-allocates, without copying its content, a memory segment of size 'Old_Size' referenced by 'Ptr'. The new size is passed a 'Size' argument.
